name: glass-dock
title: Glass Dock
description: A macOS-style liquid-glass application dock built on the refraction engine: a four-item bar with a glass lens that springs under the selected item and magnifies its icon and label. Tap an item or grab the lens and drag it along the bar; it snaps to the nearest item on release. Other items stay crisp until the lens passes over them.
docs: Import { GlassDock } from "@/components/ui/glass-dock". The bar (icons + labels) renders inside the Glass so the lens actually magnifies and refracts whatever it sits over; an invisible button layer on top handles clicks, hover, and the drag (with pointer capture, so the grab cursor stays during a drag). Pass `items` as an array of { id, label, icon, badge? }; control selection with value / defaultValue / onValueChange. The glass lens springs under the selected item and can be grabbed and dragged along the bar (snaps to the nearest item on release). Optionally pass screen content as children to sit behind the bar. Tune the material with the optional `glass` prop (a LiquidGlassConfig: mode, specular, blur, refraction { amount, thickness }, translucency, shadow); DEFAULT_LIQUID_GLASS is exported.
